message:
fix: terminal fullscreen positioning stuck in top-left corner (#47)

The Radix DialogContent has base styles: top-[50%] left-[50%] translate-x-[-50%] translate-y-[-50%].
When maximized, 'fixed inset-4' overrode top/left but the -50% transforms remained,
shifting the dialog up and left by half its size.

Fix: explicitly set translate-x-0 translate-y-0 top-4 left-4 when maximized
to override both position and transform.

Fix crash: force IPv4 DNS and catch unhandled rejections

Node.js 20 defaults to IPv6-first DNS resolution. On droplets without
IPv6 connectivity, this causes all outbound HTTPS requests (Telegram API
calls via node-telegram-bot-api) to fail with AggregateError, crashing
the process. Setting dns.setDefaultResultOrder('ipv4first') fixes this.

Also adds an unhandledRejection handler so transient network errors
log warnings instead of killing the server.

## 新增文档

### 1. 部署回归分析 (DEPLOYMENT_REGRESSION_ANALYSIS.md)
记录所有部署过程中的regression和数据丢失问题：
- 数据丢失事件 (2026-03-17)：docker-compose重建时PostgreSQL volume被重新创建
- OAuth配置丢失：使用docker run时遗漏关键环境变量
- Agent API Key不匹配：agent使用通用key而非注册后的唯一key
- 实例状态自动变为error：多个health check机制冲突

### 2. 标准化部署流程 (STANDARD_DEPLOYMENT_PROCEDURE.md)
定义正确的DevOps规则和流程：
- 部署前准备：环境检查、备份、配置验证
- 标准部署流程：本地开发 → 服务器部署 → 验证
- 紧急回滚流程：数据丢失或regression时的快速恢复
- Remote实例

message:
FIX: 修复安装脚本三个阻塞性Bug [FIX-20260318-安装脚本修复]

## 摘要

- 业务需求: 用户按照官方文档安装时连续遇到三个Bug，导致安装失败
- 技术方式: 修复sh/bash兼容性、下载路径逻辑、模块命名冲突
- 核心变更: install.sh、client.py、types.py重命名、README.md

## 问题详情

### Bug 1: sh/bash 兼容性问题
- 现象: `curl ... | sh` 报语法错误 `Syntax error: "(" unexpected`
- 原因: 脚本使用bash数组语法，但Ubuntu的sh是dash
- 修复: 添加 $BASH_VERSION 检测，给出明确错误提示

### Bug 2: requirements.txt 下载路径错误
- 现象: 所有下载源均失败
- 原因: 脚本从 src/requirements.txt 下载，但文件在根目录
- 修复: 区分 src_modules 和 root_modules，分别下载

### Bug 3: types.py 模块名冲突
- 现

fix: treat any HTTP response as healthy (503 = needs API key)

openclaw returns 503 before API key is configured. The gateway IS
running, it just can't serve requests yet. Any HTTP response means
the process is alive and listening.

fix(email): deduplicate sends, rewrite all templates

- helpers.py: 60-second dedup window in _queue_email() prevents double sends
- auth.py: welcome email rewritten with paste-to-agent message, numbered
  steps, and single CTA (Open Dashboard). No raw API endpoints.
  WELCOME_MESSAGE relay updated to reference skill.md instead of raw endpoints.
- billing.py: scale plan email rewritten with comparison table (Free vs Scale),
  warmer tone, and dashboard CTA button.

fix(ui): chat history scroll loading and pull-to-refresh suppression

- Add wheel event handler for desktop: trigger history loading when
  scrolling up at top (scroll event doesn't fire when already at top)
- Suppress pull-to-refresh on ChatPage (IM best practice: chat pages
  use WebSocket, no need for manual refresh)
- Fix empty date on session separator by using latest orphan's archivedAt
- Add "no more history" static hint when all history segments loaded
- Add debug logs for history loadin

Fix webhook crash: pass file paths instead of Buffers to telegram bot

node-telegram-bot-api expects file paths for key/cert in webHook options
(it calls readFileSync internally). The code was passing Buffers from
readFileSync, causing the library to treat the PEM content as a filename.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: disable automatic AI online integration tests

AI API tests (test-online-esp32c3, test-online-esp32s3) are inherently
flaky due to external API dependencies (HTTP 503, rate limits, transient
network failures). These cause false-negative CI results on every push
to main.

Remove push/schedule triggers from ci-online-integration.yml, keeping
only workflow_dispatch so the tests can still be run manually when
needed. Smoke tests (boot, shell, kv) remain fully automated.

Signed-off-by: Chao Liu

Open port 8443 for Telegram webhook across all deployment paths

Telegram webhook mode requires inbound traffic on port 8443, but this
port was missing from every deployment config — causing the bot to
start successfully but never receive messages from Telegram.

Fixed in: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, cloud-init.yml, packer.pkr.hcl,
and install.sh. Added .gitignore to prevent .env (with API keys) from
being committed.

Royal Heights improvements

fix: WAL remove() does a full O(n) parse after every single removal (#588) (#611)

* fix: #588 — WAL remove() does a full O(n) parse after every single removal

Track active entry IDs in an in-memory Set<string> (activeIds) seeded
once at construction. write() adds to the set; remove() deletes from it
and checks size === 0 without touching the file system. clear() and
pruneStale() keep the set in sync.

This reduces remove()'s compaction-check from O(n) (full file parse) to
O(1) (Set.size check)

fix: Windows npm.cmd cannot be executed by node.exe directly

On Windows, npm.cmd is a batch wrapper, not a JS file. Calling
`node.exe npm.cmd install` causes loader errors. Fix:
- Add downloaded Node dir to PATH so npm.cmd can find node.exe
- Call `npm.cmd install` directly instead of `node.exe npm.cmd install`
- Add error handling for OpenClaw install failure

declaration in card-holder.html caused an uncaught SyntaxError that silently
killed the entire inline <script> block, preventing loadMyCards() from ever
running — resulting in a blank "My Cards" tab.

this.messages and the finally block that clears this.loading.
The chatStore.messages watcher fired scrollToBottom, but its
$nextTick resolved while loading was still true — the v-if hid
the messages div, so scrollTo had no visible effect.

Move this.loading=false right after this.messages assignment so
both changes are batched into the same Vue DOM update, allowing
$nextTick to scrol

fix(ui): chat page shows no messages after browser refresh

When refreshing on a chat page, activateSession was called before
sessionsStore loaded, so chatSessionKey was empty. Because
__resolveBotId fell back to the first online bot, the botId check
passed and loadMessages ran immediately—only to bail out (chatSessionKey
empty) and set loading=false. This made __retryActivation's retry
condition (botId || errorText || loading) all false, so the retry
never fired.

Add a chatSessionKey guard aft

fix: defer long analyze/explore messages to LLM for archetype detection

Messages starting with "analyze" or "explore" that are 12+ words long
should go to the LLM classifier instead of being quick-classified as
research. This allows the LLM to detect market_research, data_analysis,
and other archetypes that benefit from orchestrated bespoke UI output.

fix(jsonl-parser): filter delivery-mirror audit entries from turn stats

OpenClaw writes a `delivery-mirror` entry (provider: "openclaw", model:
"delivery-mirror") into the .jsonl file each time a response is
cross-delivered to another channel (e.g. Telegram + webchat). These are
internal routing records, not real LLM turns.

Previously clawprobe counted them as billable assistant turns, inflating
turn counts, token totals, and cost estimates. The global model/provider
tracker was also being ove

Replace transcription polling with event-based wait via UpdateTranscribedAudio

which fixes premature fallback on long voice messages.

advise the agent to retry or fall back to download_media + external STT.

Architecture change: cost_records stored one row per session per day with
only cumulative input/output tokens, discarding cache token details. This
made it impossible to compute accurate costs in the summary view.

New turn_records table stores one row per assistant turn with:
  - input_tokens, output_tokens (full values reported by the model)
  - cache_read, cache_write (for discounted billing)
  - estimated_usd (pr

fix: prevent phone numbers from being misidentified as department IDs (#124)

The heuristic regex /^\d+$/ treated any pure-digit string as a party ID,
causing 11-digit phone numbers to be sent as toParty instead of toUser
(WeCom error 81013). Narrowed to /^\d{1,6}$/ so only short digit strings
are treated as department IDs; longer ones fall through to toUser.

feat(cost): add cache token billing for accurate cost estimation

Extend ModelPrice with cacheReadMultiplier and cacheWriteMultiplier fields so
estimateCost can correctly bill the three token categories that most providers
distinguish:

  cost = normal_input * input_price
       + output * output_price
       + cache_read * input_price * cacheReadMultiplier
       + cache_write * input_price * cacheWriteMultiplier

where normal_input = input - cacheRead - cacheWrite.

Provider cache rates added:

fix: fix permalink summary and og:image not appearing on landing pages

Three root causes fixed:
1. Jina Reader often returns empty (paywalls/JS-rendered sites) → Gemini
   skipped → ai_summary stored as "". Fix: pass existing news_items summary
   as fallback; Gemini now uses it if Jina returns nothing.
2. Idempotency cached broken (empty) rows on first attempt, so retries
   always returned the blank result. Fix: only short-circuit if both
   ai_summary AND og_image_url are non-empty; otherwis
Update README.md

fix(cost): accumulate input tokens per turn to match API billing semantics

The API bills each turn's full context (usage.input) as input tokens, so the
correct total input = sum of usage.input across all turns, not just the last
turn's value. Previously totalInput was overwritten (=) instead of accumulated
(+=) in parseSessionStats, causing the reported input token count to be 10-50x
too low for multi-turn sessions.

Also corrects kimi-k2.5 price to $0.60 input / $2.00 output per 1M tokens.

verify/chain to report 3 hash mismatches.

fix: use stable sessionKey for conversation lookup (#114)

* fix: use stable sessionKey for conversation lookup to prevent fragmentation

When OpenClaw recycles a session (compaction, idle timeout, overflow),
it generates a new ephemeral UUID as sessionId. LCM previously looked up
conversations solely by this UUID, creating a new conversation each time.

This change threads the stable `sessionKey` (e.g. `agent:main:main`)
through ingest/ingestBatch/bootstrap/afterTurn and uses it as the
primary

records without any DB migratio

platform: disable feishu in ESP32-S3 QEMU sdkconfig

Feishu was enabled in the S3 QEMU defaults but CI lacks valid
credentials, causing endless token retry spam.  More importantly,
CONFIG_RTCLAW_FEISHU_ENABLE gates out the AI boot test thread
(TLS memory conflict guard), so the online CI test waiting for
'[boot] AI>' always times out.

Disable feishu for S3 QEMU to match C3 QEMU and unblock CI.

fix: enqueue API now uses dispatch_queue instead of immediate dispatch (#88)

The enqueue endpoint was bypassing the auto-queue system entirely —
creating cards and dispatching immediately without touching
dispatch_queue or auto_queue_runs tables. This meant:
- No entries visible in web dashboard auto-queue panel
- Priority parameter was parsed but unused
- Queue ordering was ignored

Now enqueueToDispatchQueue() creates/reuses an active auto_queue_run
and inserts a dispatch_queue entry with the

fix: auto-detect openclaw binary name after npm install

The openclaw npm package may register its bin under a different name.
Now after npm install:
1. Check expected path (bin/openclaw)
2. Scan bin dir for anything openclaw-related
3. Read package.json bin field from node_modules
4. Log bin dir contents in error message for debugging

Also pass discovered binary path to service registration instead of
hardcoding env.openclaw_bin().

fix(ui): propagate Ctrl+C/Esc cancellation instead of returning empty string (#2757)

When p.isCancel() detected user cancellation in prompt() and
selectFromList(), the result was silently converted to "" instead of
exiting. This caused infinite retry loops in billing prompts, silent
fallthrough in oauth key entry, and unintended defaults in name prompts.

Now both functions call process.exit(0) on cancel for a clean exit.

Fixes #2745

fix: use cd for SDK npm publish instead of --prefix (#86)

## Problem

`npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk publish` was publishing the root
`@resciencelab/dap` package instead of `@resciencelab/agent-world-sdk`.
This caused a 403 (version already published) and the SDK never got
published.

## Fix

Replace `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk publish` with `cd
packages/agent-world-sdk && npm publish` to ensure npm reads the correct
`package.json`.

fix: inject gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs for K8s startup overhead (#377)

* fix: inject gateway.handshakeTimeoutMs for K8s startup overhead

OpenClaw v2026.3.12 reduced the WebSocket handshake timeout from ~10s
to 3s (GHSA-jv4g-m82p-2j93), which is too short for Kubernetes where
plugin loading adds container startup overhead. Inject a safe default
of 10000ms; user-set values take precedence.

Closes #370

fix: add NPM_TOKEN env to release workflow (#84)

`changesets/action` checks for `NPM_TOKEN` env var to detect npm auth
availability. The workflow only had `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`, causing:

```
No NPM_TOKEN or OIDC available - assuming npm is already authenticated
```

This adds `NPM_TOKEN` alongside `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` so changesets can
properly authenticate for npm publish.

every single CLI invocation, blocking for up to 10s on slow/offline

after a successful check and skips the network call if the cache is
less than 1 hour old.

fixes

fix: session history pagination — API response format mismatch

The client fetchSessionsPaginated() sends GET /sessions?page=1&limit=20
and expects { data: [], pagination: { hasMore, total } }.

The server was returning { sessions: [] } ignoring page/limit params,
causing the client to read undefined and silently fail (empty history).

Fix: when ?page param is present, return paginated format with proper
count query. Without ?page, legacy format preserved (backward compat).

implementation using FreeRTOS+TCP socket API:

The implementation mirrors the RT-Thread POSIX socket version
(claw_net_rtthread.c) with FreeRTOS+TCP API equivalent

fix(tarball): validate VM architecture when only one arch asset exists (#2753)

When a GitHub Release contains only one architecture-specific tarball
(e.g., x86_64 only), the download command now checks `uname -m` on
the remote VM and fails with exit 1 if the arch doesn't match. This
prevents installing an x86_64 binary on ARM (or vice versa) and ensures
the orchestrator falls back to live installation.

fix(ssh): always escalate to SIGKILL in killWithTimeout (#2752)

proc.killed is true as soon as kill() is called, not when the process
exits. This meant SIGKILL escalation was always skipped, leaving stuck
processes hanging indefinitely. Remove the faulty guard and always
attempt SIGKILL after the grace period — try/catch handles already-dead
processes.

net: fix GEM ISR read-clear bug, achieve DHCP on Zynq QEMU

Root cause: Xilinx BSP's XEmacPs_IntrHandler reads the GEM ISR
register twice — once as RegISR and once as RegQiISR[0] (which maps
to the same offset 0x24 for Queue 0).  Cadence GEM ISR is read-clear,
so the second read always returns 0.  This prevented emacps_recv_handler
and emacps_send_handler from ever being called.

Fix: override XEmacPs_IntrHandler in the platform's NetworkInterface.c
with a version that reads ISR only once and di

fix(plugin): await ensureAllAgentSessions in coclaw.info to fix empty session on new OC instance

新 OC 实例首次连接时，ensureAllAgentSessions 是 fire-and-forget，
coclaw.info 可能在 ensure 完成前就返回，导致 UI 后续 chat.history
获取不到 sessionId。改为在 coclaw.info handler 中 await ensure 的
promise，确保 session 就绪后再响应。

net: fix Data Abort and enable DHCP TX on Zynq-A9 QEMU

Data Abort (alignment fault) occurred in eARPGetCacheEntry when
accessing packed network structures at non-aligned addresses.
FreeRTOS+TCP uses 2-byte packet filler (ipconfigPACKET_FILLER_SIZE)
causing misaligned struct field accesses.

Root cause: QEMU enforces strict alignment even with SCTLR.A=0.
Fix: add -mno-unaligned-access to cross.ini and FreeRTOS+TCP build,
making GCC generate byte-access sequences for unaligned data.

fix(windows): use dirname() instead of unix-only regex for config paths (#2738)

The regex `configPath.replace(/\/[^/]+$/, "")` only matches forward
slashes, so on Windows (which uses backslashes) it returns the full
path unchanged. `mkdirSync` then creates `digitalocean.json` as a
directory, causing EISDIR on the next write.

Replace with `dirname()` from `node:path` which handles both separators.
Affects digitalocean.ts, hetzner.ts, and aws.ts (oauth.ts already used
dirname correctly).

fix(security): resolve symlinks in prompt file validation to prevent bypass (#2744)

validatePromptFilePath used path.resolve() which only normalizes the
string but doesn't follow symlinks. An attacker could create a symlink
(e.g., innocent.txt -> ~/.ssh/id_rsa) to bypass sensitive path checks
and exfiltrate credentials. Now uses realpathSync() to canonicalize
the path before pattern matching.

hit SSH lockout on server creation because the newly registered key landed
on page 2+ and was omitted from the ssh_keys payload.

fix(security): use writeFileSync for credential files — Bun.write ignores mode option (#2742)

Bun.write does not support the `mode` option, so credential config files
(Hetzner, DigitalOcean, AWS, OpenRouter) were created with 0644 permissions
instead of the intended 0600, exposing API tokens to other local users.

Switch to node:fs writeFileSync which correctly applies file permissions.

release: Claw-Swarm V9.0.0 — Field-Mediated Coupling Architecture

Architecture: Replace 7-layer linear hierarchy (L0-L6) with 7 autonomous domains
connected through a 12-dimensional signal field. Zero feature flags. Zero idle modules.

Key changes:
- 7 domains: core, communication, intelligence, orchestration, quality, observe, bridge
- Dual foundation: SignalField (forward-decay) + DomainStore (memory+JSON) + EventBus (27 topics)
- ModuleBase replaces MeshNode: declarative produces/consumes co
autodidact cycle + learning state

fix: image-to-research on mobile — detect MIME from magic bytes and pass MediaType to Claude

On Capacitor Android, file.type from camera capture can be empty or
application/octet-stream. This caused images to save as .bin files and
Claude to refuse analysis. Now the upload handler sniffs MIME from file
magic bytes, and the inbound handler always passes MediaType/MediaTypes
so Claude receives the correct image MIME type.

fix(core): add compaction safeguard to prevent OOM during context compaction

AgentBox crashes with OOM (2GB heap) when memory-flush's session_before_compact
hook injects a flush message during compaction. The flush triggers another agent
turn → another compaction check → infinite loop, each cycle adding entries
without reducing context until JSON.stringify exhausts the heap.

Port OpenClaw's compaction-safeguard extension which takes over the
session_before_compact hook and generates a structur

net: fix network buffer exhaustion and enable DHCP discover

Two fixes:
1. Increase ipconfigNUM_NETWORK_BUFFER_DESCRIPTORS from 32 to 60.
   The Zynq GEM DMA uses 32 descriptors for RX, leaving 0 for TX
   and DHCP.  With 60, there are 28 buffers available for TX/DHCP.

2. Fix GIC IRQ dispatch (previous commit) enabled the tick interrupt,
   allowing FreeRTOS timers to fire.  DHCP state machine now runs
   and successfully calls prvSendDHCPDiscover.

fix: prevent context bloat stalls — 5m heartbeat, compact at 35%, mandatory mid-cycle compaction

Root cause: agent context grew to 50% (132k tokens) with 0 compactions,
43 unprocessed result files piled up, gateway timed out on next event.

Fixes:
- Heartbeat interval: 10m → 5m (less idle time between cycles)
- reserveTokens: 112000 → 170000 (compaction triggers at ~35% instead of ~57%)
- keepRecentTokens: 20000 → 15000, maxHistoryShare: 0.5 → 0.35
- HEARTBEAT.md step 0a2: compact threshold low

net: fix GIC IRQ dispatch in vApplicationFPUSafeIRQHandler

FreeRTOS ARM_CA9 port reads GICC_IAR (acknowledge) before calling
vApplicationFPUSafeIRQHandler(ulICCIAR).  The handler was ignoring
the passed IRQ ID and calling XScuGic_InterruptHandler which reads
GICC_IAR again, getting 0x3FF (spurious) every time.

Fix: use the ulICCIAR value from the port to look up and call the
registered handler directly from the XScuGic handler table.

Result: tick interrupt (IRQ 29, SCU Timer) now fires correc

Before this PR:

`getAnthropicReasoningParams()` could return `{ thinking: { type:
'enabled' } }` without `budgetTokens` when `findTokenLimit()` couldn't
determine a model's token limit. The Claude Agent SDK then silently
converted this to `--thinking adaptive`, which non-Anthropic upstream
providers (e.g., cherryin proxy) do not support, resulting in a 400
error: `"thinking type should be enabled or

fix(e2e): add /usr/local/bin to openclaw PATH in verify.sh for GCP (#2736)

On GCP VMs (running as root), npm installs openclaw to /usr/local/bin
instead of ~/.npm-global/bin because the system npm prefix is writable
and already in PATH. The E2E verify_openclaw() and related gateway
helper functions only explicitly listed ~/.npm-global/bin, ~/.bun/bin,
and ~/.local/bin — missing /usr/local/bin when .spawnrc sourcing
silently fails in the piped-bash SSH exec context.

net: skip PHY setup and force link-up for QEMU Cadence GEM

QEMU's Cadence GEM emulation makes MDIO PHY operations extremely
slow (each register read/write takes many QEMU cycles).  The full
Phy_Setup sequence took longer than DHCP timeouts, preventing any
network activity.

Changes:
- Copy Zynq NetworkInterface.c to platform/zynq-a9/drivers/ to
  avoid modifying the FreeRTOS+TCP submodule
- Skip Phy_Setup entirely, force 100 Mbps operating speed
- Force ulPHYLinkStates = LINK_UP, skip prvGMACWa

feat: web search key rotation, data-driven hand routing, and health-aware LLM fallback (#1127)

* feat(web-search): support auth_profiles key rotation for Brave Search

When the primary Brave API key returns 402 (Payment Required) or 429
(Too Many Requests), the search engine now automatically rotates to the
next key configured in `[[auth_profiles.brave]]`. Previously,
web_search only read a single env var (`BRAVE_API_KEY`) and fell back
to DuckDuckGo on failure, ignoring backup keys in auth_pro

ARM64 builds use CGO_ENABLED=0, so CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE USING fts5()
crashed on startup.

- detectFTS5(): runtime probe at db open time
- Store.HasFTS5: gate FTS virtual table + trigger creation
- SearchKnowledge: FTS5 MATCH when available, LIKE fallback otherwise
- Migration order: main tables first, FTS conditionally after
- Zero impact on CGO builds (fts5

fix(vlm): add max_tokens parameter to VLM completion calls to prevent vLLM rejection (#689)

* fix(vlm): add max_tokens parameter to VLM completion calls to prevent vLLM rejection

Without max_tokens, vLLM allocates all context space to input tokens and
assigns 0 output tokens, rejecting requests with "You passed N input
tokens and requested 0 output tokens." Even when prompts fit, the model
has no guaranteed output space, leading to truncated or empty responses.

This adds max_tokens support ac

fix(deploy): auto-migrate now patches missing columns on existing tables

Root cause: when reusing a Neon database from a previous deploy, tables
already exist with an older schema. CREATE TABLE is skipped (no-op),
leaving the old table missing columns like guard_policies.agent_ids.

Fix: after DDL execution, parse each CREATE TABLE statement and run
ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for every column. This is a no-op
for columns that already exist, and adds any that are missing.

platform: fix FreeRTOS scheduler on Zynq-A9 QEMU

Three issues prevented FreeRTOS from scheduling tasks:

1. Vector table overlap: .freertos_vectors section lacked "ax" flags,
   causing the linker to assign it the same VMA as .text.  IRQ vector
   pointed at Xil_In32 instead of FreeRTOS_IRQ_Handler.  Fix: add
   "ax" flag and ALIGN(32) for ARM VBAR requirement.

2. VFP not enabled: vPortRestoreTaskContext uses VPOP to restore FPU
   context (configUSE_TASK_FPU_SUPPORT=2), but CP10/CP11 access a

fix(windows): download JS bundle instead of bash wrapper on Windows (#2730)

The bash wrapper scripts (.sh) contain bash syntax that PowerShell
cannot parse. On Windows, download the pre-built JS bundle from
GitHub releases and run it directly via `bun run {cloud}.js {agent}`,
which is exactly what the bash wrapper ultimately does.

Affects both interactive (execScript) and headless (cmdRunHeadless)
code paths. macOS/Linux behavior unchanged.

Closes #2726

Fix flaky prompt draft test that fails in CI

The test expected the textarea to be empty after remount, but the
persistence layer now intentionally flushes drafts to localStorage on
unmount. In CI (slower), the debounce also fires before unmount, making
the draft available on re-render. Fix by clearing draft storage before
the second render to test the clean-start scenario deterministically.

feat: add extension hot-reload after OTA update

Instead of waiting for browser restart, the extension now hot-reloads
immediately after downloading a new version. A ReloadCallback from
component_loader removes the old extension and re-adds from the OTA
directory, avoiding circular dependencies between browser/extensions
and browser/ocbot.
